other_observers: Kara Lewantowicz; Norm Erthal Joe Roller
specific_location: about 6.5 miles west of the intersection of the G Road and US Highway 10 along McElmo Creek in a residence.
description: This bird was an oriole based on bill shape, size, and body coloration.  The bird was yellow, mostly yellow-orange, with a a black mask, chin, and throat. It had a black back with two whitish wingbars, or shoulder patch and wingbar, the upper one much wider than the lower one. The tail was long and black. The bill was thin and long, curved throught its length. The bird was lightest yellow-orange on the belly and vent. Upper tail coverts were also orangish.Behaviors: The bird was observed mostly moving and perching in tall cottonwood trees. It was not seen feeding. It seemed to prefer the tallest trees in the area around the house in particular, but also flew across the G road and perched in cottonwoods there.Call: none heardPlumage: alternate
